risks.description: The part is a small 2.00 mm 1018 steel formed arched plate; the shop has sheet metal stamping/forming and tool-room capability, but the curved profile will require suitable tooling and springback control.
risks.mitigation: Use dedicated forming tooling/fixtures and verify first-off profile dimensions before production release.

risks.description: The 2.00 +/-0.05 mm gage requirement is achievable but depends on procuring and verifying compliant sheet material rather than only in-process machining control.
risks.mitigation: Require mill certificate/incoming inspection for SAE J1392 Grade 1018 sheet thickness and yield strength.

risks.description: The drawing calls out phosphate coating per PS-119.0 and bond prep all around part; the shop lists in-house coating/plating and cleaning capability, but the exact PS-119.0 phosphate and bond-prep qualification is not explicitly stated.
risks.mitigation: Confirm whether Almity's in-house coating/prep process is approved for PS-119.0 and the required bond-prep note; otherwise use an approved vendor.

risks.description: The 2X 1.50 lower land and 2X 5.5 A/C side land callouts were extracted as geometrically ambiguous and should be confirmed before tooling.
risks.mitigation: Clarify the drawing interpretation before final tool design or production release.

feasibility.assessment_notes: SAE J1392 Grade 1018 steel is within the shop's common carbon/alloy steel capability, and the 76.98 x 54.0 x 39.99 mm envelope fits the listed sheet-metal, VMC, and inspection capacity. No tolerance is tighter than the shop's listed careful stamping capability of 0.05 mm or machining/inspection capability of 0.02 mm; the main risks are tooling/springback control for the formed shell, incoming sheet gage verification, and confirmation of coating/bond-prep specifications.

spec_assessments.tolerance_or_requirement: Carbon steel material; as-shown minimum yield 340 MPa
spec_assessments.reasoning: The shop profile lists steel/common carbon steel materials as supported and has sheet-metal stamping/forming capability suitable for low-carbon 1018 steel.
The +/-0.05 mm gage requirement equals the shop's careful sheet-stamping tolerance capability and also depends on incoming sheet thickness control.
spec_assessments.risk_description: Thickness is controlled largely by purchased sheet gage and may vary during forming, though the drawing separately allows +/-30% variation in formed areas.
spec_assessments.mitigation: Verify sheet certificates and perform incoming thickness inspection; control forming to remain within the drawing's formed-area allowance.
spec_assessments.required_process: incoming material inspection and sheet forming

spec_assessments.reasoning: The +/-0.25 mm width tolerance is looser than the shop's normal sheet-stamping tolerance capability of 0.10 mm.
The +/-0.5 mm general tolerance is well within the shop's listed sheet-metal stamping capability.

spec_assessments.nominal_value: 76.98 mm length x 54.0 mm width x 39.99 mm height
spec_assessments.reasoning: The envelope is small relative to the shop's available sheet-metal forming capability and also fits the listed VMC inspection/support envelope if needed.
spec_assessments.shop_capability_reference: machine_inventory.sheet_metal_stamping available; vmc_3axis travel 600 x 400 x 400 mm

spec_assessments.tolerance_or_requirement: Sheet body/wall thickness for formed plate
spec_assessments.reasoning: A 2.0 mm low-carbon steel sheet is compatible with the shop's advertised sheet-metal stamping/forming capability.
spec_assessments.shop_capability_reference: machine_inventory.sheet_metal_stamping processes include stamping and forming

spec_assessments.tolerance_or_requirement: Metal thickness variation +/-30% allowable in formed areas
spec_assessments.reasoning: The +/-30% formed-area thickness allowance relaxes local thinning/thickening requirements and is compatible with a formed sheet part.
The shop lists in-house coating/plating capability, but the profile specifically names alkaline zinc plating and powder coating rather than PS-119.0 phosphate coating.
spec_assessments.risk_description: Exact phosphate coating specification approval is not confirmed from the capability profile.
spec_assessments.mitigation: Confirm in-house PS-119.0 phosphate capability/approval or plan to use an approved coating vendor.
spec_assessments.shop_capability_reference: machine_inventory.plating_line processes include plating/coating; powder_coating_line available; PS-119.0 phosphate not explicitly listed

spec_assessments.tolerance_or_requirement: Starred bond-prep note, applicability tied to part table
spec_assessments.reasoning: The shop has cleaning/finish-bench capability, but the feature graph notes uncertainty over which part-table row the starred bond-prep note applies to and does not define the bond-prep process standard.
spec_assessments.mitigation: Clarify whether the RFQ part is CM03B364360 or CM03B364361 and obtain the required bond-prep specification if applicable.
spec_assessments.shop_capability_reference: finish_bench processes include cleaning; assembly_cell processes include cleaning

feature_assessments.notes: The main body is manufacturable by sheet-metal forming/stamping in 1018 steel, but it carries risks for gage verification, forming/tooling control, and coating/bond-prep specification confirmation.

spec_assessments.reasoning: The +/-0.25 mm radius tolerance is looser than the shop's normal sheet-stamping tolerance capability, provided suitable forming tooling is used.
A 180 degree arched sheet form is within the advertised sheet-metal forming capability when supported by dedicated tooling.
spec_assessments.shop_capability_reference: machine_inventory.sheet_metal_stamping processes include forming; tool_room supports tool manufacturing

spec_assessments.spec_description: Formed area with thickness variation note
spec_assessments.reasoning: The drawing's formed-area thickness variation allowance is compatible with normal sheet forming of 2.0 mm steel.

feature_assessments.notes: The outer radius is within listed forming tolerance capability and does not create a hard feasibility issue.

spec_assessments.reasoning: The +/-0.25 mm radius tolerance is within the shop's sheet-stamping and profile-forming capability.

spec_assessments.tolerance_or_requirement: Likely concentric/parallel to outer R38.49 profile; no explicit GD&T
spec_assessments.reasoning: Producing two repeated inner/profile arcs is feasible with stamping/forming tooling and no tighter positional GD&T is specified.

feature_assessments.notes: The inner arcs are feasible; no separate concentricity tolerance or datum scheme was specified.

spec_assessments.reasoning: The +/-0.25 mm tolerance is physically achievable, but the feature graph states that the role of the 1.50 dimension is geometrically ambiguous.
spec_assessments.mitigation: Confirm whether the 2X 1.50 callout defines lower end land height, straight extension length, or another profile detail.

spec_assessments.mitigation: Confirm drawing interpretation before tool design.

clarifications.question: Which part-table row is the RFQ intended to cover: CM03B364360 with process per AS SHOWN, or CM03B364361 with PS-135.22 and the starred bond-prep requirement?
clarifications.why_it_matters: The part table shows two related part numbers with different process entries, and the starred bond-prep note may apply only to the PS-135.22 row.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use CM03B364360 / AS SHOWN, matching the RFQ file name, while retaining phosphate coat per PS-119.0 as a general note.

clarifications.question: Confirm the intended interpretation of the SECTION A-A callout '2X 5.5 A/C' and the main-view '2X 1.50' callout: are these side land/as-cut offsets and lower end land heights respectively?
clarifications.why_it_matters: These dimensions define small edge/land features and affect the formed profile and tooling definition.
clarifications.suggested_default: Interpret 2X 5.5 A/C as two 5.5 mm side land offsets in SECTION A-A and 2X 1.50 as two lower end straight land heights below the main baseline.

clarifications.question: Confirm the complete drawing number and revision, since the title-block drawing number appears as 'CM03B36436-' and the revision box is not clearly populated.
clarifications.why_it_matters: This affects document traceability, although it does not block physical manufacturability.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use drawing number CM03B36436- and revision null/blank as read from the title block.

clarifications.question: Please verify feature F005, Two transverse side lands or flanges, including whether the 2X 5.5 A/C callout was read correctly.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The feature was extracted with low confidence and could affect the detailed formed profile and die design.

clarifications.question: Is Almity's in-house coating/prep process approved for phosphate coating per PS-119.0 and any required bond-prep specification?
clarifications.why_it_matters: The shop lists coating/plating capability, but the exact phosphate and bond-prep process approval is not explicit in the capability profile.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat as feasible with risk and verify process approval before release.

assumptions.text: The RFQ is assumed to be for part CM03B364360 with process per AS SHOWN, matching the RFQ file name.
SAE J1392 Grade 1018 is treated as low-carbon steel sheet in a non-hardened condition; no heat treatment is required because none is specified.
The formed arched profile is intended to be produced as a sheet-metal formed/stamped part, consistent with the 2.00 mm gage note and formed-area thickness variation allowance.
No additional unextracted holes, threads, datum-based GD&T controls, or surface finish requirements are present.

reasoning_summary: This is a small formed 2.00 mm SAE 1018 steel arched plate, and the shop has the required steel, sheet-metal stamping/forming, tool-room, deburr, coating, and inspection capabilities. The listed dimensional tolerances are achievable, and the part fits the available machine/envelope limits. The assessment can proceed with medium risk due to forming/tooling control, incoming gage verification, ambiguous extracted land/flange callouts, and confirmation of PS-119.0 phosphate/bond-prep requirements.
